Trunk Edition. Louis Vuitton’s travel heritage is distilled into contemporary leather goods, where supple grained calfskin, suede details, and refined utility define a new language of everyday elegance — expressed in a myriad of essential accessories by @pharrell. Discover the full collection via link in bio.

#PharrellWilliams #LouisVuitton

Louis Vuitton Camionnette. From the time display in the engine through to the key housed within a remarkably miniature Monogram trunk clad in signature canvas and brass-tone hardware, each element plays its part in animating the complication. Discover the timepiece via link in bio. 

#LVObjectsofTime #LaFabriqueduTemps #LouisVuitton

Louis Vuitton Camionnette. An expression of the Art of Travel shaped by the House’s historic delivery truck, debuting as a horological masterpiece within the Objects of Time collection, crafted in collaboration with L’Épée 1839 for La Fabrique du Temps Louis Vuitton. Discover the creation via link in bio. 

#LVObjectsofTime #LaFabriqueduTemps #LouisVuitton

Espace Louis Vuitton Osaka. Presenting “Paintings and Banality”, an exhibition dedicated to American artist Jeff Koons. Bringing together seven major works — from his iconic pieces of the 1980s to large-scale paintings of the 2000s — the exhibition reveals his ability to transform the everyday into icons of contemporary art. Discover more via link in bio.

Within the Fondation Louis Vuitton « Hors-les-murs » programme, the Espaces Louis Vuitton showcase exhibitions of works from the Collection across Europe and Asia.

#JeffKoons #EspaceLV #CollectionFLV #FondationLouisVuitton #LouisVuitton

caption:
The CC LEAGUE BY CHANEL BEAUTY.
As a visionary, Gabrielle Chanel has sought body freedom because this means freedom of the spirit. Freedom that is won through movement. Today, CHANEL draws a tailor-made program to work alongside seven athletes as they explore and assert their uniqueness. The CC LEAGUE BY CHANEL BEAUTY is rooted in a deep conviction: beauty is a collective adventure and a force for transformation.
 
@reneemontgomery @gabyagundez @mj.bonnin @89c1ndy @heidigaugain @gilli_kim @allezjain @janelleleungg 
 
Directed by @charlottemwales

#CCLEAGUEBYCHANELBEAUTY

Meet the CC LEAGUE BY CHANEL BEAUTY.
They are seven, a number symbolising achievement and harmony. Each embodies a unique vision of beauty, where power and grace are recognised and expressed differently. Seven exceptional athletes and a unique collective united by the promise of a triumphant future and the ambition to become role models for their generation. Like the program’s mentor: two-time basketball champion Renee Montgomery.
 
@reneemontgomery @gabyagundez @mj.bonnin @89c1ndy @heidigaugain @gilli_kim @allezjain @janelleleungg
 
Captured by @charlottemwales 

#CCLEAGUEBYCHANELBEAUTY

Never too much denim.
Guided by COMÈTES COLLECTIVE member Valentina Li’s unique vision, the new, limited-edition DENIM MAKEUP COLLECTION reimagines denim through looks ranging from understated to bold. It is unequivocally the alpha and omega of any look, catering to every desire.

Collection and makeup by @valentina1121li, member of the #cometescollective, in collaboration with the CHANEL Makeup Creation Studio.

LOUISE TROTTER APPOINTED CREATIVE DIRECTOR OF BOTTEGA VENETA

Bottega Veneta and Kering are delighted to announce the appointment of Louise Trotter as the new Creative Director for the House, marking the opening of an exciting new chapter in the brand’s creative journey.

Louise Trotter, Creative Director of Carven, is celebrated for her ability to draw inspiration from real life and meticulous approach to craftsmanship. She will join Bottega Veneta at the end of January 2025.

firstComment:
Се именем Моим зовётся,
Не имея плод от Духа.
Так извергну поруганье,
Избавляя от страданья.
|
Ибо вскоре надлежит,
Город этот осудить.
За то что он имея чрево,
Преисполнил его гневом.
|
Стыд и срам извергнет власть,
Там где ей нога даст глас.
Ибо в духе не имеет,
Гласа Божьего творенья.
|
И да откроются врата,
Затворяя плод ярма.
Ибо вскоре надлежит,
После город оживить.
|
И откроется во Мне,
Точно возгласы в тебе.
Говоря "Мой Бог со мной,
Да будь же праведен суд мой!"
|
Ибо Бог богов не дремлет,
Избавляя от ярма.
Так будет же благословенье,
На чело что от Меня!
|
Ибо власть имеет возглас,
Чтущий солнце из зари.
И Я явлю ему Свой голос,
Вопрошая век тяжбы!
|
‭Иезекииль 6:2 - Библия📖
[2] — Сын человеческий, обрати лицо к горам Израиля; пророчествуй против них 
|
И явит он своё знаменье,
Говоря Мои слова.
Се от Бога повеленье,
Разрубать судьбы ярма.
|
И да станет же свободен,
От твоих гребущих рук.
Ибо Богом заповедан,
Чтить пути, что явит Путь.
|
И там где явится нога,
Там откроет небеса.
И да исполниться всё вскорь,
Так явит издать Господь!
|
И да отряхнёт пророк,
Подошвы ног своих святых.
Ибо нынче город сей,
Не является Моим.✝️ @yong.lixx

Harmonic complexity. The Louis Vuitton Escale Minute Repeater balances caliber complexity with the beauty of a flammé guilloché dial, its minute track echoing the House’s signature trunk brackets. Discover the creation via link in bio.

Maison Louis Vuitton Beijing Sanlitun. Where enduring savoir-faire meets architectural vision, a new chapter unfolds in the House’s long-standing collaboration with Jun Aoki, defined by a shared language of form, light, and craftsmanship, from Beijing to Tokyo, Hong Kong, and Osaka. Discover more via link in bio.

#MaisonLouisVuittonSanlitun #Beijing #LouisVuitton

Maison Louis Vuitton Beijing Sanlitun. A new lifestyle destination takes shape through a sculptural glass façade, where fluid, non-geometric contours echo traditional Taihu stones and the movement of a Spring-Summer 2016 silhouette by @NicolasGhesquiere. Envisioned by architect Jun Aoki, the space unfolds across four levels, bringing together Women’s and Men’s ready-to-wear, beauty, the Home Collection, and more, alongside a Café, extending the House’s art of living into a gastronomic experience. Discover the location via link in bio.

#MaisonLouisVuittonSanlitun #Beijing #LouisVuitton

Christian Dior’s vision of love, beautifully reimagined in an exceptional numbered edition.
Miss Dioramour, a declaration of love, whispered in silk.

#DiorBeauty #DiorParfums #MissDior

The Miss Dioramour rare piece honors Dior’s couture heritage with the iconic Marc Bohan’s 1968 ‘Dites-le avec des Fleurs’ pattern, while Francis Kurkdjian’s composition unveils the elegant floral chypre heart of Miss Dior Parfum.

#DiorBeauty #DiorParfums #MissDior

The precious bow of Miss Dioramour, entirely crafted in silk, is an exceptional piece. With precision and passion, each bow is tied by Dior’s ‘petites mains’ at the neck of the iconic bottle, celebrating the couture heritage of Marc Bohan’s 1968 pattern. 

#DiorBeauty #DiorParfums #MissDior

caption:
Some people carry keys. I carry carbs, crustaceans, and chaotic squirrels... all courtesy of @fendi. My top 5:

1) The Ravioli is al dente, on-trend, and, frankly… delicious. I always knew carbs could be couture!

2) The Crab is giving beachcore with a side of emotional damage. The sad little eyes? The woven claws? I’d protect him with my life.

3) The Fendi Ice Cream Bar is equal parts cozy and cool, like dessert in shearling form.

4) This little Squirrel is clearly up to something, and I love that for him. 10/10 would follow into the woods and hand over my snacks.

5) I have to end with the French Fries: Never greasy, always golden. These fries are high fashion and judgment-free.

Which one would you carry?!
firstComment: all of them! but the crab is sooooo cute!!! 😻

Dior Autumn Winter 2026-2027.
 
The Jardin des Tuileries, and its socio-historical importance, provided both an inspiration and setting for the show. The centuries-old park in the centre of Paris has always been a stage for seeing and being seen. Its emblematic green chairs are referenced for the seating and architecture of the show space built around a lily pond.

caption: In 2016, I became a mom. Our business was still growing, I was thrilled for the next chapter, and I experienced the highest highs and lowest lows that year. I had a doctor misdiagnose my daughter as having a serious, life altering issue when she was still in utero. My life was forever changed, in so many ways, and I don’t remember the style so much that year (however, we posted so many flat lays!!), but I remember all that I went through physically and mentally! I am endlessly thankful for 2016 as being one of my greatest teachers, but man, it was a rough one!

Mark Zuckerberg doesn’t just “own watches”... he collects grails.

firstComment:
Title: Toxic Company with Zero Work-Life Balance

Cons:
	•	A lot of politics. The manager openly favors female employees he has personal relationships/affairs with, and if you don’t cooperate with them, you face bias.
	•	No work-life balance — minimum 12 hours of work daily.
	•	Extreme micromanagement — every minute is tracked, and you are forced to do tasks outside your role. Saying “no” can cost you your job.
	•	No HR or management team to raise concerns. The manager controls everything.
	•	Invasion of privacy — personal and family matters are interfered with during emergencies.
	•	Unprofessional communication — the manager dominates conversations, twists words, and uses them against you.
	•	No job security. Senior employees pressurize juniors and speak in a demeaning tone.
	•	Incentives not paid for four months. Why hire if you can’t afford to pay staff?
	•	Company exploits both employees and customers — totally unethical.

Pros:
	•	None.

Advice to Management:
Hire a proper HR team, stop favoritism, pay employees on time, and build a fair, ethical work culture.

The Fondation Louis Vuitton at Espace Louis Vuitton New York in collaboration with the J. Paul @gettymuseum. In « Jeune Homme à sa fenêtre » (« Young Man at His Window », 1876) Gustave Caillebotte captures a moment of quiet contemplation within a newly modernized Paris. With its cinematic framing, the artist depicts his younger brother René gazing onto the city from their Rue de Miromesnil apartment — reflecting both the allure and solitude of modern life, and offering a striking exploration of identity, distance, and the gaze. Reserve a ticket via link in bio. 

Gustave Caillebotte at Espace Louis Vuitton New York, October 28 — November 17, 2025
Louis Vuitton 57th Street, 6 East 57th Street, New York

#LVArtsCultureProgram #EspaceLV #NewYork #FondationLouisVuitton #LouisVuitton

From Cannes to the Grammys...

In May 2018, Penélope Cruz unveiled her Atelier Swarovski collaboration at the 71st Cannes Film Festival, debuting a collection of synthetic jewels.

That same year, Emma Watson turned the Oscars afterparty into a statement, wearing a mix of ethically sourced and lab-created accessories from Vrai & Oro, Ana Katarina, and Ana Khouri.

Later in 2018, Lady Gaga arrived at the London premiere of A Star is Born in an Alexander McQueen gown, paired with ethically sourced cluster earrings by Anabela Chan.
Fast forward to 2023, Billie Eilish attended the Grammys wearing lab-grown diamond jewelry

Red carpet or not, lab-grown diamonds belong in every story. Find yours in The Diamond Edit at theluxurycloset.com.
firstComment:
Title: Toxic Company with Zero Work-Life Balance

Cons:
	•	A lot of politics. The manager openly favors female employees he has personal relationships/affairs with, and if you don’t cooperate with them, you face bias.
	•	No work-life balance — minimum 12 hours of work daily.
	•	Extreme micromanagement — every minute is tracked, and you are forced to do tasks outside your role. Saying “no” can cost you your job.
	•	No HR or management team to raise concerns. The manager controls everything.
	•	Invasion of privacy — personal and family matters are interfered with during emergencies.
	•	Unprofessional communication — the manager dominates conversations, twists words, and uses them against you.
	•	No job security. Senior employees pressurize juniors and speak in a demeaning tone.
	•	Incentives not paid for four months. Why hire if you can’t afford to pay staff?
	•	Company exploits both employees and customers — totally unethical.

Pros:
	•	None.

Advice to Management:
Hire a proper HR team, stop favoritism, pay employees on time, and build a fair, ethical work culture.

caption:
Copenhagen Fashion Week might officially be our new favorite for bag spotting!  All the images are now up on our site and Substack (don’t sleep on it, we saved some gems just for there). Here are five I can’t stop thinking about:

1. This @loewe Puzzle bag + fire-engine-red nails = the color/texture combo that has me in a chokehold. I’m not free, and I don’t want to be.

2. A black-and-white striped Chanel 22 I’ve never seen in the wild before?! Yes, please. And paired with Chanel sunnies? The audacity. The excellence.

3. Forget minis, this @balenciaga is going full vintage and unapologetically oversized. Big bag energy only, especially in a bright color!

4. Remember @gucci x Adidas? Neither did I… until this bag came strutting by, reminding me that extra is still everything.

5. This gradient jacket (tan melting into green) next to a silver Chanel 25? Yeah, I’m spiraling. Clearly, the universe is telling me I *need* a 25. 

📸 by @claireguillon #copenhagen #copenhagenfashionweek #bagsinthewild

firstComment:
Como todo plato casero, la cazuela chilena es adaptable: puede ser de cordero, pollo o cerdo, pero la más clásica es la de res. Lo que no puede faltar es el trozo de carne, papa, calabaza (zapallo) y si es verano un trozo de elote (choclo) por persona. En mi familia se disfruta invierno y verano. Un platillo realmente reconfortante.
Pilar Hernandez Actualizada Sep 23, 2015
Guardar
Comparte
Mantener la pantalla
Ingredientes
1
lb de brisket (tapapecho)
2
cucharadas de aceite vegetal
1
cebolla cortada en 4 pedazos
1
pimiento morrón cortado en 4 pedazos, sin semillas
1
cucharadita de orégano
1
cucharadita de comino
sal y pimienta,
ramita de apio
2
elotes cortados en 3
2
zanahorias peladas y cortadas en 3
6
papas rojas pequeñas peladas
4
puñados de arroz
6
trozos de calabaza naranja
1
puñado de ejotes cortados a la francesa
5-6 tazas de agua hirviendo
Instrucciones
1
Corta la carne en 6 porciones. Calienta aceite en una cacerola amplia. Dora la carne 3 minutos por lado. Agregar la cebolla, los aliños, sal y pimienta, revuelve y cocina por 5 minutos.
2
Agrega las papas, zanahoria, apio, calabaza y cubre todo con agua hirviendo, 5-6 tazas. Cocina por 10 minutos. Agrega el arroz y el elote, cocina por 8 minutos más. Agrega los ejotes picados y cocina 3 minutos más.
3
Prueba, corrige la sazón y sirve caliente. En cada plato coloca 1 trozo de carne, 1 papa, 1 pedazo de calabaza, 1 elote.
4
Sirve con cilantro picado espolvoreado encima.

firstComment:
Title: Toxic Company with Zero Work-Life Balance

Cons:
	•	A lot of politics. The manager openly favors female employees he has personal relationships/affairs with, and if you don’t cooperate with them, you face bias.
	•	No work-life balance — minimum 12 hours of work daily.
	•	Extreme micromanagement — every minute is tracked, and you are forced to do tasks outside your role. Saying “no” can cost you your job.
	•	No HR or management team to raise concerns. The manager controls everything.
	•	Invasion of privacy — personal and family matters are interfered with during emergencies.
	•	Unprofessional communication — the manager dominates conversations, twists words, and uses them against you.
	•	No job security. Senior employees pressurize juniors and speak in a demeaning tone.
	•	Incentives not paid for four months. Why hire if you can’t afford to pay staff?
	•	Company exploits both employees and customers — totally unethical.

Pros:
	•	None.

Advice to Management:
Hire a proper HR team, stop favoritism, pay employees on time, and build a fair, ethical work culture.

sculptures by Magdalene Odundo.

Dior Winter 2026-2027 by Jonathan Anderson.
 
Outside the legendary Dior address at 30 Avenue Montaigne, a commemorative plaque honours the early 20th-century couturier Paul Poiret — a quiet reminder that his boutique once stood only moments away. Known for his fluid forms and global influences, Poiret helped establish Avenue Montaigne as the epicentre of couture. In a city long defined as a melting pot of artists, writers and designers, this unexpected dialogue invited further exploration for Dior Creative Director Jonathan Anderson.
 
LaKeith Stanfield, Yu Shi, Louis Garrel and Henry Lau uncover Poiret’s Paris.
 
#DiorWinter26

In this cobalt silence, she rests not out of ease but intention. Her boots speak first, defiant and deliberate. Her stillness? A kind of possession. This is where elegance pauses, and power reclines—undisturbed, unbothered, unmistakably hers.

firstComment:
⸻

Felt Misled – Same Price as New Bags
We bought four second-hand bags from The Luxury Closet after seeing ads claiming 50–90% discounts on all luxury items. But when we checked the prices at the Louis Vuitton store, we were shocked to find the same bags selling at the exact same price—brand new. We feel completely misled and disappointed. These are old, used bags, not worth what we paid. Buyers, please double-check real prices before purchasing. Very disappointed with this experience.

Hi Dior HQ,

I would like to make a formal complaint against Dior Singapore Beauty Head Of Department Abby for wrongful termination. I was a part timer engage by a agency in singapore for dior Christmas popup event at singapore changi airport terminal 1 from 22 nov-1jan. As a top salesman rank number 2 in sales result, i have been wrongfully terminated by Abby for unsubstantiated misconduct. 

On 25 dec my sales was “snatch” by my colleague and i confronted her infront of customer that resulted to the escalation of investigation. 

There was no inquiry provided by Dior singapore and i was terminated with immediate effect. 

This was what was given to me as the reason for termination. 

it was clear that xxx had a dispute with the customer and displayed unprofessional behavior and a poor attitude in front of the customer.

we have decided to immediately suspend xxx working hours for today.am and consider they worked until 5pm only. 

In addition, all of xxx shifts from today onwards will be cancelled, and we will not be continuing with him.

I would like to seek redress from Dior HQ international for a dispute resolution for this incident thank you. 

..

CHANEL’s cross-town line–presented in New York, Matthieu Blazy’s first Métiers d’art collection features a joyful cavalcade of personalities, seen through a filmic lens. Art Deco extravagances, elegant tweeds pushing the boundaries of savoir-faire, sophisticated embroideries and fantastic animal prints, the collection shifts through time and space.

The Métiers d’art collections are unique to CHANEL. Every year since 2002, they celebrate the exceptional French savoir-faire at the heart of the House’s creations. This annual rendezvous is a declaration of love to the artisans who contribute to CHANEL collections all year round.

firstComment: Chanel is a decent brand in the fashion industry, but it often engages in very tacky practices by constantly hiring celebrities for advertising. I value a brand for its craftsmanship, not because it has hired some internet-famous celebrities, singers, or actors. Stop all this, Chanel. You deserve to be a unique and proud brand.

Louis Vuitton is deeply saddened by the passing of Frank Gehry, whose visionary talent and long-standing collaboration have left an indelible mark on the Maison.

From the Fondation Louis Vuitton to several of our emblematic flagships and creations, his work stands as a testament to his extraordinary imagination and continues to inspire us.

We extend our deepest condolences to his family, his teams, and all those touched by his talent.

Photo ©️ Mario Kroes

My constant quest for a great everyday bag has been met with a winner: the @ysl Jamie Shopping Bag! 

Pros:
- The nappa leather is divine! I’m talking when you open the box, you are immediately hit with the smell (like a new car smell) and it is so soft, and luxurious and begging to be touched! 
- The drawstring ties allow the bag to be cinched or expanded which not only changes the silhouette but also adds security for your items inside
- This bag has every element that denotes it as a YSL bag (obviously, with the logo) but still feels different 

Cons:
- The bag is on the heavier side! It is most likely due to the chain strap and the leather, but I could see this becoming too heavy if you over-stuff it. The site lists this bag as lightweight, but I’d call it more medium weight
- The interior can be a bit of bottomless pit. Again, this is why I use pouches all the time! It’s also a black cotton lining, again making it hard to find things inside if you don’t use pouches and organization!

Overall: 
I have loved carrying this bag and am so happy to have a big bag to add to my repertoire! After all, I always need a bag that can carry all the items I need, and one that feels and looks this good while doing it is a major plus! Shout out to @gabriellasmith for the perfect backdrop!

Silk flowers 🌹 @wangsoderstrom give a zingy twist to @gianpaolo_pagni 's Botanica Grafica carré. 🪷​
Let your senses bloom. 💐​​​

The story behind: From Arumfel to Centummorbia, Eupatorium, Loc Sumutri, Spatula foetida and Zurumbeth, the most erudite readers may recognize these species from the index of “The Herball or Generall Historie of Plantes”, the 16th-century botanical manual that Gianpaolo Pagni used as the basis for his new herbarium. Like planting seeds, he superimposed botanical drawings and stamp prints to create a graphic vocabulary that only he knows the secret of. One flower grew, then another, then one more, until there were twelve on each side of this double-sided scarf. While the flowers blend with the words on the front, they fade away on the back, leaving a graphic, colorful bouquet to blossom on the silk.

​​
#GraphicBouquet​​​
#AwesomeBlossom​​​
#BotanicaGraficaDoubleFaceCarré

Starting a new Sunday series with a look at the bag I used for the week and the bag I am switching to for the upcoming week! #sundaybagswitch

This week I carried the Dior Toujours Bag. I’ve shared it a few times in stories and received a ton of questions! #diortoujours 

- This is the Medium in black crinkled calfskin with Macrocannage topstitching and black hardware detailing. 
- The interior includes a matching pouch which is removable and I love it! 
- The leather handles can be adjusted to carry the bag by hand or over the shoulder. Game changer!
- There is no zip top closure but a leather strap closure is included which helps secure the bag
- There is also a sleek CD lock closure which twists to connect and change the bags shape
- I’ve really loved carrying this bag. It’s lightweight, fits far more than you’d expect, and looks great

I’m switching to the Louis Vuitton Neverfull in Epi for this week. A true classic and workhorse of a bag, let’s see how it is! Check back next Sunday for a look at my week with the bag!
firstComment: Can a 13 inch laptop fit inside this bag?

Swinging carré. ✨🪁 Since 1937, the Hermès carré, first imagined by Robert Dumas, keeps on swinging. Discover this harmony of Grand Tralala carrés, designed by @jamin.virginie, captured by @sarahblais.

The story behind: In the 19th century, the steeds of the Hungarian Royal Guard sported a ceremonial harness with gleaming buckles, like this framed bridle with its delicate leather straps. Beneath bands of gradient colors, Virginie Jamin's delicate lines are endlessly intensified and repeated, giving this emblematic equestrian motif a new rainbow.
